title: Cluster record persistence in OpenSearch
description:
**As a** memory system, **I want** cluster metadata stored as special documents in OpenSearch, **so that** cluster state can be queried alongside item documents without a separate datastore.

Cluster records (centroid embedding, p95 distance, label, member count) are stored as documents in the same OpenSearch index with a `record_type: "cluster"` field discriminator. This avoids the need for a second index or an external cluster store.

The OpenSearch client gets three new methods:
- `persist_clusters(clusters)`: bulk-index cluster records
- `get_cluster(cluster_id)`: retrieve a single cluster record
- `get_all_clusters()`: retrieve all cluster records (filter by `record_type: cluster`)

## Key Files

- Modify: `plexus/analysis/opensearch_client.py` (add cluster record methods)
- Modify: `plexus/analysis/opensearch_client_test.py`

## Dependencies

- Story plx-b02d58 (Bulk index with blue/green alias swap)
- Story plx-941008 (Cluster from pre-computed embeddings)

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Feature: Cluster record persistence in OpenSearch

  Scenario: Cluster records indexed alongside item documents
    Given 5 clusters with centroids, p95 distances, and labels
    When persist_clusters(clusters) is called
    Then 5 documents are bulk-indexed with record_type = "cluster"
    And each has fields: cluster_id, centroid_embedding, p95_distance, label, member_count

  Scenario: Cluster retrievable by ID
    Given cluster records have been persisted
    When get_cluster(cluster_id=3) is called
    Then the returned record has cluster_id = 3
    And contains centroid_embedding, p95_distance, label, and member_count

  Scenario: All clusters retrievable
    Given 5 cluster records have been persisted
    When get_all_clusters() is called
    Then 5 cluster records are returned
    And none have record_type = "item"
